summary: Plato: Timaeus — source for demiurge and numerical_mysticism
description:
**Parent:** Corpus Expansion Tier 1 (child 1 of 8)

## Source

- **Translator / license:** Benjamin Jowett, 1871. Clean US-PD.
- **URL:** https://www.sacred-texts.com/cla/plato/timaeus.htm
- **Structure:** single HTML page, ~188KB, one continuous `<PRE>` block of speaker-tagged prose. No in-line section markers.
- **Format:** `html`
- **Chunking:** `paragraph-group`, `paragraphs_per_chunk = 6-8`, `max_tokens = 800`. `regex-section-split` is NOT viable — no numbered markers exist in the body.
- **Pre-strip:** trailing `[N]` footnote block.

## Concept payload

`demiurge` (the canonical creator-craftsman source), `numerical_mysticism`, world-soul, cosmogony. Upstream of the Gnostic demiurge already in corpus.

## Done contract

`acquire.py --only plato-timaeus` produces clean raw; `chunk.py` writes `corpus/platonism/plato-timaeus/chunks/*.toml`; smoke-test one chunk reads as substantive Timaeus prose, not boilerplate.

## Ref

`docs/corpus-expansion-candidates.md` §3.1

resolution.note: Acquired 178,613 chars from sacred-texts.com; chunked to 50 paragraph-group chunks (avg 762 tokens). Manifest entry + chunking config landed; same shape as plato-symposium. Sources floating concepts demiurge + numerical_mysticism. Embed deferred to a bulk-pass alongside other Tier 1 children.
